title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During the investigation into Elena Marshall's death, Mrs. Gardner suggests that the deceased was a strange woman and speculates that her death might lead to a reconciliation between Mr. Marshall and a former childhood sweetheart. Mr. Gardner adds that Mrs. Marshall spent much of her time with a young man named Redfern, while describing Mr. Marshall as a reserved man.

Major Barry, drawing on his military experience in the Himalayas, compares the case to a past incident in Shimla where a man strangled his wife during a rage. He suggests that Mrs. Marshall was a woman who easily seduced men, including Redfern. When questioned about his whereabouts, Major Barry claims he went to Saint-Louis to make a phone call regarding a horse bet at the post office, but asserts he got lost in the alleyways on his return. Colonel Weston and Chief Inspector Colgate consider the possibility that Barry used a boat to travel to Elfin Cove to commit the crime and return, using the story of getting lost as an alibi. The police decide to have Inspector Corghetti investigate the route of Barry's vehicle.

Later, Emily Bostwick expresses a lack of sympathy for the deceased, labeling her a "thoroughly bad woman." She claims Mrs. Marshall had a history of ruining those she was involved with, citing a cousin's connection to a scandal involving an inheritance and a young man who engaged in unsavory stock market dealings to fund his relationship with her. Bostwick concludes that Mrs. Marshall's history makes the investigation of her death straightforward.
